title: Enforce lockfile regeneration gate

sources.reason: Task 4 requires a lockfile regeneration gate.
Committed generated lockfile that must remain reproducible.
Release workflow is the blocking publication gate.

riskAssessment.reason: A stale lock can make release evidence and CI installation non-reproducible.

executionDecision.reason: The lockfile input and generated output are local and testable.

preReviewWarnings: Confirm regenerated lock bytes are compared before release publication.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Release workflow regenerates requirements-dev.lock from requirements-dev.in with hashes and fails on byte drift.
A regression test asserts the regeneration gate and existing require-hashes installation behavior.
Distribution references document the lockfile reproducibility gate.
guidelines: Do not weaken --require-hashes installation or existing supply-chain checks.

intent.problem: The workflow installs the committed lock but does not prove it is reproducible from the committed input before publication.
constraints: Do not change dependency selections in this Work Item.
Do not hand-edit generated lock content.
intent.rationale: Comparing a fresh pip-compile output prevents stale or manually altered lockfiles from entering a release.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Committed lockfile exactly matches fresh hash-enabled generation.
scenarioCoverage.reason: Workflow regression test and release gate.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Lockfile drift fails before release publication.
scenarioCoverage.reason: The comparison command exits non-zero on mismatch.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the complete remediation, PR merge, and release workflow.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
